cards.back: A traditional teaching method where the teacher writes equations on the blackboard and explains the solution orally.
A visual presentation software used to create engaging and interactive slides, often used to support lectures and discussions.

cards.front: Lecturer's role in chalk and talk method
cards.back: The lecturer writes equations and diagrams on the blackboard and guides the students through the solutions orally.

cards.front: Advantages of chalk and talk method
cards.back: The chalk and talk method allows for real-time feedback, promotes active listening, and encourages students to ask questions.

cards.front: Disadvantages of chalk and talk method
cards.back: The chalk and talk method can be time-consuming, lacks visual aids, and may not cater to different learning styles.
